steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left of blank: independent clause ending with “...in Kyoto.” Right of blank: dependent clause (“when brothers Andre and Edouard Michelin created the guide in 1900”) followed by an independent clause (“it was little more than a marketing gimmick—a way to increase profits...”).

steps.title: Apply the semicolon-conjunctive adverb rule
steps.content: A semicolon separates two independent clauses, with a conjunctive adverb like “however” (followed by a comma) showing the contrast between them. “Kyoto; however,” correctly ends the first clause and introduces the second statement—which contains a dependent temporal clause followed by its main independent clause. Choices B and D lack the contrast word “however,” while C places “however,” after a comma instead of the required semicolon. That matches choice A — A is correct.
